title: Resource Management and Configuration — Quick Check

questions.q: Name three concrete API improvements pathlib.Path offers over os.path.
questions.a: 1. `/` operator for joining paths instead of os.path.join(). 2. `.read_text()` / `.write_text()` replacing open() boilerplate. 3. `.suffix`, `.stem`, `.parent` as attributes rather than function calls.

questions.q: Why are context managers mandatory for all resource lifecycles, not just file I/O?
questions.a: Context managers guarantee the `__exit__` / cleanup path runs even when exceptions occur, preventing resource leaks (file handles, DB connections, network sockets, temporary directories) regardless of how the code exits.

questions.q: What is the correct layered priority order for a CLI script's configuration, from highest to lowest?
questions.a: CLI flags > environment variables > config file > built-in defaults. This allows deployment overrides without code changes while keeping sensible defaults for local use.

questions.q: Why should secrets never appear in a config file committed to source control, even in an encrypted form?
questions.a: Encrypted secrets still leak the encryption key problem; key rotation requires code changes; and git history preserves plaintext if the secret is accidentally committed even once. Environment variables or a dedicated secrets manager decouple secrets from the code lifecycle entirely.
